creator: emz@norma.perm.ru
text:
I've stepped onto this on 14.0, the troubled route was attached to the openvpn tun0 interface. Restarting the openvpn clears the address on the interface, but still keeps the route:

tun0: flags=8002<BROADCAST,MULTICAST> metric 0 mtu 1500
        options=80000<LINKSTATE>
        groups: tun
        nd6 options=29<PERFORMNUD,IFDISABLED,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>

# route -n get 10.8.0.0/24
   route to: 10.8.0.0
destination: 10.8.0.0
       mask: 255.255.255.0
        fib: 0
  interface: tun0
      flags: <UP,DONE,PINNED>

Note that deleting the tun0 interface makes the route orpaned:

[root@ronin:log/openvpn]# netstat -rn | grep 10.8 
10.8.0.0/24        link#24            U           ---

and even then it's impossible to delete it:

# route delete 10.8.0.0/24
delete net 10.8.0.0 fib 0: gateway uses the same route

The most astonishing thing is that recreating the tun0 interface with another subnet pins the route back to it !

# route -n get 10.8.0.0/24
   route to: 10.8.0.0
destination: 10.8.0.0
       mask: 255.255.255.0
        fib: 0
  interface: tun0
      flags: <UP,DONE,PINNED>
 recvpipe  sendpipe  ssthresh  rtt,msec    mtu        weight    expire
       0         0         0         0      1500         1         0 
# ifconfig tun0           
tun0: flags=1008043<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,MULTICAST,LOWER_UP> metric 0 mtu 1500
        options=80000<LINKSTATE>
        inet 10.9.0.1/24 broadcast 10.9.0.255
        groups: tun
        nd6 options=29<PERFORMNUD,IFDISABLED,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>
        Opened by PID 21675

No surprise it still cannot be deleted.
